author: ethdreamer
content: Random question I've been wondering.. is it valid for both `execution_payload_bid.value` and `execution_payload_bid.execution_payment` to be nonzero? So the true value of the bid would be the sum of these two?

author: jvranek
nflaig
content: this shouldn't be allowed. what's the use case for having both? that just makes it really hard to track this onchain if payments can come via the payload and the builder payment. is anyone asking for this or made an argument for why this is useful?

author: jvranek
content:
I assumed bc if I'm an untrusted builder whose `max_execution_payment` is capped and I still need to keep bidding higher to win the auction I could by adding more to `bid.value`. 

you can argue that in that case I should only use `bid.value`, but I think when possible I'd prefer to not touch my on-chain stake

author: potuz
content: this is absolutely fine, for a proposer that takes a maximum trusted bid of 1ETH a builder may want to send them 1ETH trusted and 0.5ETH trustless

author: potuz
content: There is absolutely no problem in allowing both values to be non-zero

author: potuz
content: the protocol only considers the trustless part

author: ethdreamer
content: Yeah it shouldn't be any harder to track on chain given that both values are clearly on chain. I just wanted to clarify that this is acceptable and thus when comparing the value of the bid, the true value is the sum.

author: terencechain
content: Yep. I thought about that more of a "feature" than a bug

author: nflaig
content: this is definitely the case, probably can be solved on the tooling layer easily, but I am just skeptical that this will be useful, and just because something in theory is possible, doesn't mean we need to support it. it's low complexity so sure if everyone thinks this will find usage we can support it

author: ethdreamer
content: I can make the beacon api PR for submitting the `BuilderPreferences` ahead of time.. just wondering.. do you guys send just a list of `BuilderPreferencesRequestV1` or do you add the `pubkey` as well? From where I am in my implementation, it seems it could be useful to add the pubkey as well..
